frameworks.name: activity-silent / hidden-state working memory
frameworks.one_line: unattended WM items held in sub-threshold neural states not directly observable via MVPA

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: EEG-based decoding used to track feature representations across the WM delay

frameworks.name: selective storage of task-relevant features
frameworks.one_line: view that task-irrelevant object features are simply deprioritized and not stored

core_question: Are task-irrelevant features of a single object stored in working memory in a hidden, sub-threshold state?
key_assumptions: WM items can occupy different activation states depending on attention
hidden representations can be momentarily reawakened by a task-irrelevant flash
MVPA on EEG indexes active feature-specific WM content
manipulations: task relevance of object feature (orientation vs color remembered across blocks)
task-irrelevant flash stimulus presented during the delay period
key_findings: orientation was decodable throughout the delay only when task-relevant
flash-evoked signals supported above-chance decoding of both task-relevant and task-irrelevant orientations
interpretations: task-irrelevant object features may be retained in a hidden WM state, not discarded
